title: Shinjis backstory
wikitext:
[]: Shinji's backstory             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jun 04, 2021 8:28 am For being the main character and the main PoV character besides Misato, Shinji sure has a lot of question marks in his past that are vaguely alluded to & sufficiently implied, but never wholly shown, and I have seen surprisingly few attempts to explore this further in fanworks. A lot of ppl just assume that he was dropped off with the teacher guy right after Yui's death, and that was the last he saw of him, but if you really look at it, there are a lot of things that hint at a more complex narrative here. I'll be talking specifically about the OG series timeline here, not the manga or the rebuilds which all have their own unique tweaks going on. But there are a lot of hints scattered through eps 1, 16, 15 and 20 that are in a very fragmentary, out-of-context fashion with no defining statement/narrative, as is typical of early/ traumatic memories. One angle that everyone picked up on (because it's hinted more clearly in 21 and explicitly stated later in EoE) is that Yui was the mastermind and stayed willingly ("but mother was smiling") Episode 1: - Fuyutsuki mentions that Shinji and Gendo haven't seen each other in three years - three , not ten. - We get the famous flashback of baby Shinji crying at a train station. The script notes that he's supposed to be "4 or 5" - not , as in the ep 21 depiction of the activation element, around 3. -> He probably rode the train on his own which is how the train car came to symbolize 'hell' for him, cause his little self sitting there with his bag? That's the traumatic moment he is "stuck" in, so to speak. He experienced/conceptualized this as rejection & grew up building much of his personality around fear of rejection. Episode 15 - We get some elaboration on the "three years" thing: Apparently when Shinji was about 10, they met up for the visit to Yui's grave & in went poorly, accumulating in Shinji "running away", and after that point, Gendo never showed up again - implying that, before this point there was at least intermittent contact. Not enough for Shinji to feel like he wasn't completely forgotten, but I'd assume he made the occasional courtesy visit. - Shinji has a notable reaction when he notices Rei sitting in the heli Episode 16 - "but you were the one who ran away!" - Gendo (explicitly labeled as from 10 years ago): "You musnt't run away" (hinting that this is were he might've picked up the phrase) - some newspaper clippings & the like of how Gendo apparently ended up on trial after the fatal accident (maybe Shinji accepted this narrative when he was older and bitter and went looking through the old papers for answers, but part of him remembers Yui being all excited about the experiment) - the newspaper clippings also include the words "out of control" so it's possible that EVA 01 went out of control for a bit during it, like in ep 14 when unit zero nearly absorbs Shinji Episode 20 - more or less where the penny comes as close to dropping as it ever does. - at this point it's very much made explicit that Shinji always lowkey blamed him for what happened to Yui (this once, he's actually innocent) - "that must be it, he left me cause Rei was there" - then the 'you left yourself' point is reiterated, but more interesting, we get to see baby Rei, in that same sepia flashback hue, the exact same outfit, and she seems to be at the train station though that could just be about fitting her in the existing metaphor. Now of course you could argue that they didn't want to come up with another design, but why make her appear as a child at all? No, what this might suggest is that Shinji actually saw child Rei with his own eyes at some point. - "No, that's not right I already knew EVA! That's why I ran away from father and mother " EP 21 - Shinji only shows up once or twice as a a cute happy toddler, but we get some further context like - Mini Rei was around in 2010 - Yui was the mastermind & deliberately brought Shinji to the activation experiment, which he witnessed - Gendo definitely actually cared about Yui & was super thrown out by her death to the point of just vanishing for a week There's also this interesting bit from the same Q & A that gave us the name meanings (linked in the wiki) 5.2) What happened to Shinji when he was small? (ep.21) He witnessed Yui's fatal accident, and "ran away" from NERV and his father. Probably as a result of this and Yui's death, Rei is recruited as a pilot. In the earlier episodes he has no recollection of this. We were made to assume that Gendou abandoned him to work on his project. Shinji finally remembers in ep.(20? 21?). I don't think this justifies what Gendou does to his son, but that's a different question.

[]: Re: Shinji's backstory             Quote Post by Sefirot Tree Of Life » Thu Jun 10, 2021 10:58 am I find it so strange how Shinji, despite being the main character, the protagonist, and poster boy of this franchise, who has had his mind itself deconstructed and analyzed multiple times, has so little detail on his background. We get extremely little information on his past, most of which is related to Gendo’s abandonment of him. Yet there is still an entire TEN YEARS of Shinji’s life we know next to nothing of. Who took care of him? Who was his teacher? Was it only one teacher? Was it dozens over the years? Was it a relative? What’s the relatives connection to his parents? It’s genuinely shocking how little information we have of this time period, but honestly, I truly do not doubt Shinji does not even remember this time of life. He’s called it boring, where nothing happened right? I can legitimately imagine Shinji from a young age was stuck in the past, remembering his mother and his father, and just drifted through life, completely disconnected from the people around him. But still, there’s a whole ten years of his life, it definitely should have had a massive impact on him, very sad we don’t know any more about this time period. Your write up was very through and well written! Some things it clarified for me: Shinji being abandoned around a year or two after Yui’s death; not in the immediate aftermath. It adds more context to Gendo: He got stuck with his wife’s sudden death, then got stuck with a bs trial about it, a trial that may have made his own son suspicious that he murdered Yui - I can understand why Gendo abandoned Shinji. He was in a terrible state of mind, could probably barely take care of himself, and Shinji may have already hated him. Rei being created in the aftermath of Yui’s death - I always figured this, but her being designated as a Pilot BEFORE Asuka, this makes sense, was something I was always trying to figure out. Shinji seeing Rei at his Mom’s Grave: I always suspected that there was something that made Shinji run away: My main being Gendo brought some combination of Naoko, Ritsuko, or Rei. Considering as you point out, l Shinji’s complex and accusations of Rei “replacing” him, it now seems very likely the only other person Gendo brought to Yui’s grave to meet Shinji, let alone a woman, was Rei Ayanami herself. Great write up!

[]: Re: Shinji's backstory             Quote Post by Derantor » Thu Jun 10, 2021 10:49 pm Really great post, Kendrix! I hope to add more in the future, but right now, I want to say that Shinji seeing Rei when he was 10 is very unlikely, as he has no recollection of her at all when he arrives in Tokio 3. If she was the reason his meeting with his father went sour, he'd remember her.

[]: Re: Shinji's backstory             Quote Post by dzzthink » Fri Jun 11, 2021 12:04 pm oh yeah, good point. I never thought that Shinji's recurring train dream could actually be related to the moment he was abandoned at the train station. I guess it's more obvious when you re-watch ep 16. It would be interesting if he did meet Rei there at that point, which is why she appears sometimes in his dream. Could mention this in the wiki

[]: Re: Shinji's backstory             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jun 11, 2021 7:54 pm i was thinking that after some refinement discussion this might or might not make a good theory & analysis article

Article: Shinjis backstory
, very sad we don’t know any more about this time period. Your write up was very through and well written! Some things it clarified for me: Shinji being abandoned around a year or two after Yui’s death; not in the immediate aftermath. It adds more context to Gendo: He got stuck with his wife’s sudden death, then got stuck with a bs trial about it, a trial that may have made his own son suspicious that he murdered Yui - I can understand why Gendo abandoned Shinji. He was in a terrible state of mind, could probably barely take care of himself, and Shinji may have already hated him. Rei being created in the aftermath of Yui’s death - I always figured this, but her being designated as a Pilot BEFORE Asuka, this makes sense, was something I was always trying to figure out. Shinji seeing Rei at his Mom’s Grave: I always suspected that there was something that made Shinji run away: My main being Gendo brought some combination of Naoko, Ritsuko, or Rei. Considering as you point out, l Shinji’s complex and accusations of Rei “replacing” him, it now seems very likely the only other person Gendo brought to Yui’s grave to meet Shinji, let alone a woman, was Rei Ayanami herself. Great write up!
